key_departures.impact: Led Washington's EDGE rotation after Dorance Armstrong's ACL tear. Signed 2yr/$11M with Titans to reunite with HC Robert Saleh. Was best of a thin EDGE group in 2025 but lacked ideal size/length. Departure is softened by the Oweh/Chaisson/Omenihu additions.
Led team with 9 sacks in 2025 at age 36 on a 1-year deal. Aging veteran bridge body — departure expected and planned around. Now replaced by Oweh/Chaisson.
11-time All-Pro turned 36 this summer. Was expected to depart as team pivoted younger. Departure directly triggered Leo Chenal signing. Hole at LB existed pre-departure; now partially addressed.

Veteran starting TE tore his ACL in December 2025. Departure created a hole at TE entering free agency. Washington entered FA with only John Bates and Ben Sinnott at the position. Okonkwo signing directly addresses this void.
RFA not tendered; signed 2yr/up to $12M with Jaguars. Left Washington with only Jacory Croskey-Merritt at RB under contract. Created a clear need at RB that Rachaad White partially addresses.
Starting center signed 3yr/$30M with Chargers. Creates a void at center that Washington has not yet addressed in free agency.
Signed 1-year deal with Eagles. Rotational CB body, not a starter-level loss. CB room remains thin regardless.
Released as a cap casualty ($18.5M max cap savings). Was not a full-season contributor in 2025 due to injury history. Release added to CB need urgency.

signings.analysis: Marquee signing of the offseason. 4yr/$100M with $68M guaranteed at age 27 — this is Washington's guy for the long haul. Oweh posted 17.5 sacks over his last two seasons combined and set a Chargers playoff record with 3 sacks in a Wild Card game. The $25M AAV ranks 9th among all EDGE rushers. With Oweh as the cornerstone, Dorance Armstrong returning from ACL, Chaisson, and Omenihu as depth, EDGE goes from Washington's most critical need to a fully addressed position. No longer a premium draft target.

Coming off a breakout 2025 season with 7.5 sacks and 74 pressures (tied 11th in NFL). 1-year prove-it deal at $11M with $10.3M guaranteed signals high confidence from both sides. At $11M AAV for a 26-year-old former first-round pick on the rise, this is high-starter-tier value. Paired with Oweh, this EDGE group goes from the team's worst unit to a potential strength. The 1-year structure means they'll reassess, but in 2026 Chaisson functions as a co-starter.

1yr/up to $7M (base $4M) depth addition. Former Super Bowl champion who had 7 sacks in 2023 with KC before a torn ACL in January 2024 limited his 2025 output to 1 sack in 6 games. High upside as a recovery candidate. Adds length (6-5) and power dimension to a unit that now has Oweh (speed/athleticism), Chaisson (burst/motor), and Omenihu (power). Depth signing only — need already addressed by Oweh/Chaisson.

Best available LB in free agency, per multiple analysts. 3yr/$24.75M with $12.4M guaranteed at age 25 — two-time Super Bowl champion from KC who brings speed, blitzing ability, and versatility in Daronte Jones' new scheme. At $8.25M AAV, this is mid-tier LB money for a player with legitimate upside. Chenal has never played more than 53% of snaps but was underutilized in a crowded KC rotation; he's expected to step into a featured role in Washington alongside Frankie Luvu. LB goes from an urgent need (Bobby Wagner departing) to addressed.

3yr/$24M ($25.5M max with incentives). Guarantee structure not yet fully confirmed — OTC shows $0 fully guaranteed but total guarantee pool likely in the $10-12M range per market comps (noted as TBD). Returns to Washington where he was originally drafted in 2018. Joins Daron Payne (contract year) and Javon Kinlaw. Washington's interior DL had just 8 combined sacks in 2025 — Settle adds a penetrator. However, he had only 1 sack in 12 games last year due to a foot injury; his 2024 was better (5 sacks). Partially addresses IDL need — team still likely wants youth there given Payne's contract situation.

2yr/$16M with $9.3M guaranteed. Robertson can play slot or outside, adding versatility alongside Mike Sainristil and Trey Amos. Washington entered FA with effectively only those two CBs guaranteed on the roster — a critical shortage. Robertson's $8M AAV is mid-tier CB starter money. Two-year commitment shows real investment, but ESPN notes the team still needs to add more at CB. Partially addresses an urgent need; CB remains a priority in the draft.

2yr/$14M. Young, 24-year-old box safety who logged 266 tackles over the last two seasons — second-most among all DBs in that span behind only Budda Baker. Brings physicality and run-support the Commanders' secondary lacked in 2025. $7M AAV is mid-starter range for safety. The 2-year deal at age 24 signals real investment. Washington already returns Will Harris and Quan Martin (both UFA in 2027), giving them strong safety depth. S need addressed.

3yr/$30M with $16.7M guaranteed (per Substack breakdown; Spotrac shows $30M base). Ian Rapoport called him the best TE available. At $10M AAV Okonkwo is firmly in starter TE territory. 26-year-old with 194 career catches and four consecutive seasons of 450+ receiving yards. Brings a 'move TE' skill set the Commanders have been missing — excels in the slot, outside, and after the catch. Three-year commitment with strong guarantees means this is their TE1 for the foreseeable future. Fills the Zach Ertz void and then some as a receiving weapon.

1-year deal (exact value not confirmed per AP — estimated at veteran-depth level based on Spotrac avg salary data and comparable signings). Former Arizona State teammate of Jayden Daniels. Had 1,539 yards from scrimmage in 2023 but became a change-of-pace back behind Bucky Irving in Tampa. Strong pass-catcher (205 career receptions, 89.1% catch rate). Fills a roster need but doesn't solve the RB starter question — Jacory Croskey-Merritt is expected to be the lead back. ESPN notes 'the Commanders still need more at this position.' Need minimally addressed at best.

Historic 2yr/$60.2M extension with $61.5M guaranteed (including record $32.5M signing bonus — largest ever for an OL). First offensive lineman to earn $30M/year. Keeps Jayden Daniels' blindside locked up through 2028. Tunsil is a 5-time Pro Bowler playing at a Pro Bowl level despite the team's 5-12 record. LT is now a non-need for Washington through at least the 2028 draft cycle.

2-year deal (exact terms not fully confirmed — estimated based on available reports). Three-time Super Bowl champion who entered the league as a 7th-round pick. 34 career starts, primarily at guard. Provides interior line depth but at depth-tier AAV, does not solve the IOL starter problem left by Tyler Biadasz's departure to the Chargers. Center vacancy remains unresolved.

2yr/$6.65M extension. Versatile swing OL who has played guard and tackle during his three seasons with Washington, including 3 postseason starts in 2024. Retained as a quality OL depth piece, but at depth AAV he is not a solution for the Biadasz (C) void. IOL/C need persists.
1yr/$7M (up to $11M with incentives). Re-signed backup behind Jayden Daniels for his third season with the franchise. QB depth secured — no need at the position created or addressed.
1-year re-sign at depth money. Veteran depth DE behind Oweh, Chaisson, Omenihu, and Armstrong. EDGE need already fully addressed. This is pure roster depth.
Re-signed on a 1-year deal. Former 49ers 2nd-round pick with upside (6 career sacks) retained as developmental depth. No meaningful impact on need picture.
1-year re-sign. Veteran interior depth alongside Payne, Kinlaw, Settle, and Newton. Depth-tier signing only.

1yr/$4M re-sign. Former first-round pick (18th overall, 2022) who played with Okonkwo at Maryland and was a depth piece for Washington in 2025. 63 career receptions, 829 yards, 2 TDs. At depth-tier money on a 1-year deal, this does not address the WR2 need alongside Terry McLaurin. Washington swung and missed on Alec Pierce and Romeo Doubs. WR remains a critical draft priority.
Re-signed swing tackle/extra lineman. Played 35 games with 6 starts over 3 seasons in Washington. Purely roster depth.
RFA not tendered; re-signed on a 1-year deal. Former 3rd-round 49ers pick with 78% career field goal percentage. Handled kicking duties in Washington; retained at reasonable cost.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Oweh (4yr/$100M, $68M gtd) is the clear long-term starter. Chaisson (1yr/$11M, $10.3M gtd) is a co-starter with 7.5 sacks last year. Omenihu is depth. Armstrong returns from ACL. Five edge rushers on roster. EDGE drops from the team's most critical need to luxury pick territory. Day 3 developmental selection at most.

Chenal (3yr/$24.75M, $12.4M gtd) is a legitimate starter at 25 years old. He joins All-Pro Frankie Luvu and Jordan Magee. Bobby Wagner's departure was absorbed cleanly. However, Chenal has never been a full-time starter and the group lacks proven depth behind the top two. Still a reasonable mid-round draft target but no longer an urgent need.

Nick Cross (2yr/$14M) is a 24-year-old tackling machine (266 tackles in last 2 seasons). He joins Quan Martin and Will Harris as a three-safety group. Safety is fully addressed and not a draft priority.

Okonkwo (3yr/$30M, $16.7M gtd) is a legitimate starter at 26 who led Titans in receiving in 2025. Three-year commitment at $10M AAV means Washington has their TE1 through the 2028 draft cycle. TE drops off the board as a need.

Tunsil's 2yr/$60.2M extension ($61.5M gtd) is the most important move of the offseason for Washington. He is now locked in through 2028 as the NFL's highest-paid offensive lineman. LT is a non-issue. The opposite OT spot (RT) was not a need heading in and remains unchanged.

Robertson (2yr/$16M, $9.3M gtd) adds a third starting-caliber CB alongside Sainristil and Amos. The Lattimore release and Marshon Jones departure left this room critically thin; it's now manageable but not deep. ESPN explicitly notes Washington 'needs to keep adding to this spot.' CB remains a high-priority draft need in rounds 1-3.

Settle (3yr/$24M) adds a penetrator to a group that had only 8 combined sacks in 2025. He joins Daron Payne (final contract year, trade candidate), Javon Kinlaw, and Jer'Zhan Newton. Interior DL is improved but not elite. Payne's contract situation (2026 is his last year) means a DT/IDL in the mid-rounds remains sensible.

Tyler Biadasz departed to the Chargers (3yr/$30M). Washington has not signed a starting center. Allegretti ($4M AAV) and Wylie ($3.3M AAV) are guards/swing players, not center replacements. The starting C vacancy is unaddressed. IOL/C escalates to the team's top remaining need entering the draft.

Washington missed on Alec Pierce ($114M to Colts), Romeo Doubs ($70M to Patriots), and Isaiah Likely (TE). Treylon Burks (1yr/$4M) is a depth re-sign only. Terry McLaurin remains the only legitimate starter. WR2 opposite McLaurin is unchanged as a critical need. Brandon Aiyuk is reportedly expected to sign a 1-year deal after being released by SF — if that happens, update accordingly. As of today, WR is still an urgent Day 1-2 draft target.

Rachaad White (1yr, depth money) provides pass-catching depth behind Jacory Croskey-Merritt. ESPN notes team still needs more. Both signings are short-term; no long-term RB solution in place. RB stays a mid-to-late round draft priority.

key_additions.headline: Elite 27-year-old pass rusher (17.5 sacks over last 2 seasons) ends Washington's EDGE crisis with a record-setting commitment
Pro Bowl LT becomes NFL's first $30M/year OL, locking in Jayden Daniels' blindside protector through 2028
Breakout pass rusher (7.5 sacks, 74 pressures in 2025) slots in as Oweh's running mate on an overhauled edge
Two-time Super Bowl champion and best available LB fills Bobby Wagner's void with speed and blitz versatility
Top TE on the market gives Jayden Daniels his first true playmaking weapon at the position after Zach Ertz tore ACL
Washington original returns home to bolster interior DL that managed just 8 combined sacks in 2025
Versatile slot/outside CB adds a critical third starter to a cornerback room that was dangerously thin entering free agency
High-motor 24-year-old box safety (266 tackles in last 2 seasons) brings physicality to a secondary that missed too many tackles in 2025
Arizona State teammate of Jayden Daniels reunites as a pass-catching RB complement to Jacory Croskey-Merritt

key_losses.headline: Starting center departs in free agency, leaving Washington without an obvious replacement at a key interior OL spot
Veteran TE departed after tearing ACL mid-season, directly triggering Washington's pursuit of Chig Okonkwo
11-time All-Pro LB's departure accelerated Washington's pivot to youth, leading directly to the Leo Chenal signing
Washington's best available edge rusher in 2025 cashes in with Titans, but Oweh and Chaisson represent a significant upgrade
Cap casualty release deepened Washington's CB room shortage that Robertson partially addresses
RFA not tendered; departure left Washington with one RB under contract before Rachaad White signing
